q1_question: What specific, directly-adaptable code patterns from OpenClaw's source can WorkTrain implement quickly to meaningfully improve -- across any part of the system?
q3_priors: The competitive analysis identified three urgent areas (worktrain doctor, unified event schema, pluggable context assembly) but OpenClaw has 80+ source directories. The quick-win patterns likely extend well beyond those three. OpenClaw is battle-tested at scale (369k stars, 35k commits/90days) so any subsystem they've hardened is worth reading carefully.
q4_good_enough: For each pattern found: enough source detail to write a GitHub issue with clear acceptance criteria and a concrete implementation sketch. At least 8-10 distinct patterns across different parts of WorkTrain (not just daemon). Patterns should be extractable without importing OpenClaw's infrastructure.
q5_out_of_scope: Channel adapters (WhatsApp, Telegram, etc.) -- not relevant to WorkTrain
OpenClaw-specific config formats that don't translate
q6_source_preferences: Primary: GitHub source via gh CLI. Focus on: src/daemon/, src/agents/, src/tasks/, src/context-engine/, src/trajectory/, src/sessions/, src/commitments/, src/acp/, src/cron/, src/security/, src/tools/. Read actual implementation files, not just type definitions.
